title: Censoring-aware time-to-next-pit model, then convert back to next-lap risk
summary: This develops the earlier hazard/survival idea into a directly testable target.
rationale: This develops the earlier hazard/survival idea into a directly testable target. The next pit is inherently a time-to-event problem with right-censoring for rows after the final stop. Survival models are built for exactly this setup, whereas plain binary labels discard timing information.
implementation_hint: For each driver-race sequence, derive `laps_until_next_pit`; use right-censoring when no future pit exists. Train an XGBoost AFT model or Random Survival Forest. Convert predicted survival/hazard at horizon 1 lap into a sidecar probability, or use predicted remaining laps to next stop as an additional OOF feature for the main classifier.
expected_effect: Should sharpen predictions around pit windows by using the full future stop-time structure instead of a one-step label only.
risk: If the conversion from survival output to 1-lap probability is noisy, CV may not improve despite a sensible event-time model.
